top_signals.title: Iran and US agree deal to open Strait of Hormuz and extend ceasefire
top_signals.summary: Adversaries to sign agreement on Friday for reopening of waterway and end to US naval blockade of Iranian ports
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, December 2025
top_signals.summary: Personal income increased $86.2 billion (0.3 percent at a monthly rate) in December, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $75.7 billion (0.3 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $91.0 billion (0.4 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $90.2 billion in December. Personal saving was $830.8 billion in December, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of disposable personal income-was 3.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Talk can follow Echo home in St James’s Palace
top_signals.summary: NO HORSE has attracted more attention ahead of this week’s five days of Royal Ascot than George Boughey’s BOW ECHO, who runs in Tuesday’s Group One St James’s Palace Stakes (4.20pm). top_signals.summary: TODAY’s Coventry Stakes (3.05pm) is the most prestigious two-year-old event at Royal Ascot and will see over 20 of the best juveniles scorch down the Ascot straight. top_signals.title: Put hands in Ur pocket for O’Brien runner in Ascot Stakes
top_signals.summary: A ROYAL favourite heads the market for the Ascot Stakes (5.00pm) in Reaching High, but at the current prices he looks worth taking on. top_signals.title: Play Opera to hit all the right notes in the Queen Anne
top_signals.summary: ROYAL Ascot kicks off with the Queen Anne Stakes (2.30pm) featuring several high-class performers including Godolphin’s favourite, Notable Speech. William Buick’s ride heads the betting after an impressive victory in the Lockinge, but there could be reason to oppose him at the prices.
